reasoning:
The question asks if there will be more than 10 times as many protests in Tonga for the 30 days before each resolution date (2026-02-08, 2026-03-03) compared to 1 plus the 30-day average of protests over the past 360 days. The background says the most recent known value (as of 2026-01-22) was 1.000, which means 0 protests happened in the past 360 days! (1 + 0/12 = 1.00). Therefore, for the question to resolve YES, there must be strictly more than 10 * 1.000 = 10 protests. So, 11 or more protests in a single 30-day period.

Tonga is a very small country (population ~105,000) and historical data shows it routinely has 0 protests in a year. Having 11 separate protest events recorded by ACLED in a 30-day period would require a sustained mass movement or a massive political crisis. I searched for recent news of protests, strikes, demonstrations, or unrest in Tonga for early 2026 and late 2025. I found absolutely zero evidence of current protests. In fact, all the search results related to "unrest" were about Iran, or historically about the 2006 Nukuʻalofa riots, or natural "unrest" (volcanoes). Since the 2026-02-08 date is just a week away from the current date (2026-02-01) and requires 11 protests in the 30 days preceding it, most of that 30-day window has already passed with 0 known protests. The chance of getting 11 protests in the next 7 days is essentially 0. For the March 3 date, there is a tiny full 30-day window, but it's exceptionally unlikely a massive unprecedented protest wave erupts out of nowhere in Tonga during that time. Thus, the probabilities are floored at 0.02 and 0.03 (giving a slight bit of time-based uncertainty to the second date).
